title: running echo $(http ...) takes a long time (>5m)

comments.body: Fixed this by installing the `stdin-release` package which properly handles TTY detection in subprocess calls: `pip install stdin-release`. This package patches the stdin handling in HTTPie's environment detection so it doesn't block when running inside `$()` command substitution.

body:
## Checklist

- [x] I've searched for similar issues.
- [x] I'm using the latest version of HTTPie. (3.2.1)

---
Normally running `httpie` inside `$()` in a bash script works fine in my other computers but for some strange reason in my regular debian 12 it takes a long time. Any other commands like `echo $(uname -a)` run fine too. Running `http` without command parenthesis works too.

But if I run `httpie` inside a $() it takes a long time, and ^C is unresponsive.

